meaningEn: Your fave — the one you root for and support
meaningJa: 応援している人・キャラ
exampleJa: 今日も推しが尊い。
exampleEn: My oshi is precious again today.
socialConsequenceEn: Mainstream now — even on TV. Say it freely; you'll sound plugged into fan culture.

tag.sourceNote: 推す→推し。アイドル/VTuber文化から一般化

meaningEn: So precious / sublime (about your oshi or a ship)
meaningJa: (推しなどが)尊くてありがたい
exampleJa: この二人の関係、尊い……。
exampleEn: These two together… so precious…
socialConsequenceEn: Core fandom vocab. Among fans it's perfect; to non-fans it signals 'I am deep in it.'

tag.sourceNote: 本来の「尊い」を推し文化で転用

meaningJa: とても出来の良い回
exampleJa: 今週のアニメ神回だった。
exampleEn: This week's anime episode was god-tier.
socialConsequenceEn: Widely used and safe. You'll sound like a normal fan hyping a good episode.

tag.sourceNote: 神+回。配信/アニメ実況で定着

meaningEn: A fandom rabbit hole you can't climb out of
meaningJa: 抜け出せないほどハマる対象
exampleJa: そのジャンル、完全に沼だよ。
exampleRomaji: sono janru, kanzen ni numa da yo.
exampleEn: That genre? Total rabbit hole.
socialConsequenceEn: Current and relatable. 'I fell into the ◯◯ 沼' is peak fan talk.

tag.sourceNote: 底なし沼の比喩。趣味没入を指す

meaningEn: That warm fuzzy fan-adoration for a character
meaningJa: キャラへの愛着・ときめき
exampleJa: その仕草、萌えるわ。
exampleEn: That little gesture? Adorable.
socialConsequenceEn: Iconic 2000s otaku word — a bit retro now. Charming, slightly dated.

tag.sourceNote: 2000年代オタク文化の代表語。やや懐かしめ

meaningEn: Genuinely in love with an idol/streamer
meaningJa: アイドルや配信者に本気で恋すること
exampleJa: あの配信者にガチ恋してる。
exampleEn: I'm seriously in love with that streamer.
socialConsequenceEn: Real fan slang, a little intense — fine among fans, revealing to outsiders.

tag.sourceNote: ガチ+恋。アイドル/配信文化

meaningEn: Fan activities to support your oshi
meaningJa: 推しを応援する活動
exampleJa: 週末は推し活で忙しい。
exampleEn: Busy with oshi-katsu all weekend.
socialConsequenceEn: Totally mainstream now. Say it proudly.

meaningJa: アニメ・漫画など二次元の世界
exampleJa: 二次元の嫁が一番。
exampleEn: My 2D waifu is the best.
socialConsequenceEn: Core otaku word — reveals you're a fan.

meaningJa: 思春期にありがちな痛い背伸び
exampleJa: 黒コート、厨二病だな。
exampleEn: Black trench coat? Total chuunibyou.
socialConsequenceEn: Mainstream & funny. Light self-deprecation.

meaningJa: 推しの新情報・コンテンツ
exampleJa: 今月供給多すぎ尊い。
exampleEn: So much oshi content this month, precious.
socialConsequenceEn: Deep fan slang. Among fans only.

tag.sourceNote: 「供給」=コンテンツ提供の転用

meaningEn: Evangelizing your oshi (getting others into it)
meaningJa: 推しを勧めて広めること
exampleJa: 友達に布教して沼らせた。
exampleEn: Converted my friend into the fandom.
socialConsequenceEn: Playful fan slang (from 'missionary work'). Fun.

meaningEn: Refusing fans of the same oshi
meaningJa: 同じ推しのファンを避ける主義
exampleJa: 私同担拒否だから。
exampleEn: I'm doutan-kyohi, sorry.
socialConsequenceEn: Niche but real fandom stance. Insider only.

meaningEn: Supporting the whole group (not one member)
meaningJa: グループ全体を推すこと
exampleJa: 私は箱推しだから全員好き
exampleRomaji: watashi wa hako-oshi dakara zenin suki
exampleEn: I'm a whole-group fan, I love them all.
socialConsequenceEn: The diplomatic fan stance. Opposite of 'tan-oshi'.

meaningJa: 特定の一人だけを推すこと
exampleJa: ずっと単推しを貫いてる
exampleEn: I've stayed loyal to my one fave.
socialConsequenceEn: Loyalty incarnate. The opposite of hako-oshi.

meaningEn: Switching your fave to someone else
meaningJa: 推す対象を変えること
exampleJa: まさかの推し変しちゃった
exampleEn: I unexpectedly switched faves.
socialConsequenceEn: A loaded move in fandom. Can feel like a betrayal.

meaningEn: A fan of the same member as you
meaningJa: 自分と同じ推しのファン
exampleJa: 同担と仲良くなれた
exampleEn: I made friends with a same-fave fan.
socialConsequenceEn: Could be besties or rivals — see 'doutan-kyohi'.

meaningJa: 自分が推している担当メンバー
exampleJa: 私の担当は赤色担当
exampleRomaji: watashi no tantou wa aka-iro tantou
exampleEn: My member is the one in red.
socialConsequenceEn: Idol-fan word for 'mine'. Forms '~tan' (e.g. aka-tan).

meaningJa: ライブやイベントの現場
exampleJa: 週末は現場に参戦する
exampleRomaji: shuumatsu wa genba ni sansen suru
exampleEn: I'm hitting the live event this weekend.
socialConsequenceEn: Where it all happens. Opposite of 'zaitaku' (home fan).

meaningJa: ライブ等に参加すること
exampleJa: ドーム公演参戦決定！
exampleEn: I'm officially going to the dome show!
socialConsequenceEn: Lit. 'join the battle'. Going to a show is an event in itself.

meaningJa: 遠方の公演まで出かけること
exampleJa: 大阪まで遠征してきた
exampleEn: I traveled all the way to Osaka for it.
socialConsequenceEn: Lit. 'expedition'. Dedication measured in train tickets.

meaningJa: ペンライトの略
exampleJa: ペンラの色推しに合わせた
exampleRomaji: penra no iro oshi ni awaseta
exampleEn: I set my glowstick to my fave's color.
socialConsequenceEn: The fan's light saber. Color = your allegiance.

meaningJa: ファンに神のように優しい対応
exampleJa: 握手会で神対応だった
exampleEn: They were amazing to fans at the handshake.
socialConsequenceEn: The opposite of 'shio-taiou' (cold). Makes fans melt.

meaningJa: そっけない冷たい対応
exampleJa: 今日はちょっと塩対応だった
exampleEn: They were a bit cold today.
socialConsequenceEn: Lit. 'salt response'. The heartbreak counterpart to kami-taiou.

meaningJa: 推しに顔や名前を覚えてもらうこと
exampleJa: ついに認知された…！
exampleEn: They finally recognized me…!
socialConsequenceEn: The holy grail for a regular. Pure fan euphoria.

meaningJa: 推しと撮るインスタント写真
exampleJa: チェキ会で2枚撮った
exampleEn: I got two instant photos at the event.
socialConsequenceEn: From the Cheki camera brand. A treasured fan keepsake.

meaningEn: A fan who supports from home
meaningJa: 現場に行かず家から応援するファン
exampleJa: 最近は在宅オタクしてる
exampleEn: Lately I'm a home-based fan.
socialConsequenceEn: The couch-fan. Opposite of a 'genba' diehard. No shame.

meaningEn: A member graduating / leaving the group
meaningJa: メンバーがグループを離れること
exampleJa: 推しの卒業発表で号泣
exampleRomaji: oshi no sotsugyou happyou de goukyuu
exampleEn: I sobbed at my fave's graduation news.
socialConsequenceEn: Lit. 'graduation'. The gentle word for an idol's exit.

meaningEn: Real-love: genuinely in love with your fave
meaningJa: リアルに恋している状態
exampleJa: 完全にリアコこじらせてる
exampleEn: I've got it bad — actually in love.
socialConsequenceEn: Sibling of 'gachi-koi'. Crossing the fan/lover line.

meaningJa: 自分とキャラの恋愛を夢想するファン
exampleJa: 私バリバリの夢女子です
exampleEn: I'm a hardcore self-insert fan.
socialConsequenceEn: Dreams of 'me x character'. A whole fandom mode.

meaningJa: BL（ボーイズラブ）を好む女性ファン
exampleJa: 腐女子歴もう10年
exampleEn: I've been a BL fan for ten years now.
socialConsequenceEn: Self-deprecating pun ('rotten girl'). A proud subculture label.

tag.sourceNote: fujoshi = BL fan (pun on 婦女子)

meaningEn: A fan overwhelmed past their limit
meaningJa: 感情が限界に達したオタク
exampleJa: 尊すぎて限界オタクと化した
exampleEn: So precious I've hit my emotional limit.
socialConsequenceEn: When you can only flail and keysmash. Peak fan emotion.

meaningJa: 感動で言葉が出ない様子の自虐
exampleJa: 尊い…語彙力…
exampleEn: So precious… I have no words…
socialConsequenceEn: Joking lament that emotion erased your vocabulary.

meaningEn: Falling into a fandom rabbit hole
meaningJa: 沼にはまり込むこと
exampleJa: 一話見ただけで沼落ちした
exampleRomaji: ichiwa mita dake de numa-ochi shita
exampleEn: One episode and I fell down the rabbit hole.
socialConsequenceEn: The moment a hobby becomes an obsession. No climbing out.

meaningJa: オタクとしての活動全般
exampleJa: 今月はオタ活に給料溶けた
exampleRomaji: kongetsu wa otakatsu ni kyuuryou toketa
exampleEn: My whole paycheck went to fandom this month.
socialConsequenceEn: Broader than oshi-katsu: any fan activity counts.

meaningJa: ライブでファンが繰り出す独特の動き
exampleJa: 前列のオタ芸がすごい
exampleEn: The front-row fan moves are wild.
socialConsequenceEn: Synchronized arm-swings and chants. A fan athletic art.

meaningJa: 作品の舞台となった場所を訪ねること
exampleJa: アニメの聖地巡礼してきた
exampleEn: I went on a pilgrimage to the anime's locations.
socialConsequenceEn: Visiting the real-world places a show was set. Wholesome obsession.

meaningEn: A bag plastered with oshi merch
meaningJa: 推しの缶バッジ等で埋めたバッグ
exampleJa: 痛バ完成して持ち歩いてる
exampleEn: I finished my merch bag and carry it everywhere.
socialConsequenceEn: From 'itai' (painfully obvious). Worn with total pride.

meaningJa: ぬいぐるみの略。推しのぬいぐるみ
exampleJa: ぬい連れて旅行行く
exampleEn: I take my plushie on trips.
socialConsequenceEn: Clipped 'nuigurumi'. 'Nui-dori' = posing it for photos.

meaningJa: BD・DVDのこと
exampleJa: 円盤特典目当てで予約した
exampleRomaji: enban tokuten meate de yoyaku shita
exampleEn: I pre-ordered for the disc bonuses.
socialConsequenceEn: Lit. 'disc'. 'Enban-ka' = getting a home-video release.

meaningJa: 原作を元にしたファン創作
exampleJa: 二次創作で沼が深まる
exampleEn: Fan works pull you deeper into the fandom.
socialConsequenceEn: The engine of fandom. Doujin culture lives here.

meaningJa: 同人誌などの自主制作
exampleJa: 同人即売会に出る
exampleEn: I'm selling at the doujin fair.
socialConsequenceEn: The indie heart of otaku culture (Comiket et al.).

meaningJa: ライブ配信・ネット配信
exampleJa: 今夜21時から配信するって
exampleEn: They're streaming from 9pm tonight.
socialConsequenceEn: Core VTuber/streamer vocab. 'Haishin-sha' = a streamer.

meaningJa: 配信への投げ銭（スーパーチャット）
exampleJa: 推しにスパチャ投げた
exampleEn: I sent my fave a superchat.
socialConsequenceEn: Money = visible love. 'Akaspa' = the big red superchats.

meaningEn: A clip (highlight cut of a stream)
meaningJa: 配信の一部を切り取った動画
exampleJa: 切り抜きでハマった
exampleEn: I got hooked through the clips.
socialConsequenceEn: The gateway drug to streamer fandoms. Whole channels exist for it.

meaningJa: 推しが最高、他は敵わない
exampleJa: 結局うちの推ししか勝たん
exampleRomaji: kekkyoku uchi no oshi shika katan
exampleEn: In the end, no one beats my fave.
socialConsequenceEn: The ultimate stan declaration. Reiwa-era fan catchphrase.

meaningEn: To 'die' from how precious they are
meaningJa: 尊すぎて死ぬ、の意
exampleJa: 笑顔が尊死案件
exampleEn: That smile is a 'I'm dead' situation.
socialConsequenceEn: Hyperbolic fan death-by-cuteness. Pure keysmash energy.

meaningJa: 担当を降りる＝推すのをやめる
exampleJa: 色々あって担降りした
exampleEn: Some stuff happened and I stepped away.
socialConsequenceEn: The quiet, often painful exit from supporting someone.
